findings.summary: retry loop sleeps 20s after the 30th failed attempt before exiting 1 — cosmetic, costs 20s of runner time on the failure path only

retry budget ~10 min vs observed 5-8 min first-publish propagation lag is thin headroom — consequence now benign (idempotent re-dispatch instead of a bricked republish); subsequent-version propagation is typically much faster so this mostly cannot recur

ruledOut: quiet-success via the exit-status idempotency predicate: both misclassification directions demonstrated to fail LOUDLY downstream (false-not-published -> republish conflict fails the step; false-published -> content-asserted retry exhausts + canary never runs); npm view exit semantics verified empirically on npm 10.8.2 and 11.18.0 against the real registry (missing version/package -> exit 1)

proof-cannot-fail: assert script extracted verbatim from HEAD YAML via PyYAML and executed under bash -e with stubbed npm — absent arm exhausts 30 attempts and exits 1; quiet-wrong-version and quiet-empty-stdout arms also exit 1; visible arm passes attempt 1; propagation-lag arm passes at attempt 4 with tee truncation preventing stale-content false pass
shell correctness: line-continuation inside if condition, pipefail necessity under GitHub bash -e {0}, tee clobbering, seq quoting, exit paths — all exercised in the stub harness
scope: single commit touching only the two claimed steps of publish.yml; both merge-base and HEAD blobs parse clean with PyYAML
future-release masking: skip path still runs the full fresh-runner canary (install from real registry + version/init/add-list/MCP-handshake content assertions), catching a visible-but-broken tarball
protocols: no AI attribution in branch commits; no secrets in diff; version interpolation inert via prove's strict semver validation
